title: Technical Ceramics from Saudi Alumina
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 2 — Technical ceramics manufacturer JV using Ma'aden alumina as feedstock
executive_summary:
Technical ceramics — alumina, silicon carbide, zirconia, and silicon nitride components used in electronics, defense, and industrial applications — are manufactured from mineral precursors that Saudi Arabia is beginning to produce domestically. Ma'aden's alumina refinery at Ras Al-Khair produces smelter-grade alumina for aluminum production; technical ceramics require higher-purity alumina, but the refining technology is directly adjacent.

The applications are diverse and high-value: alumina ceramic substrates for electronics (used in Saudi Arabia's semiconductor ambition, SC-1 through SC-4), ballistic armor tiles for defense vehicles (LS-3 supplement), wear-resistant components for oil and gas equipment, and cutting tool inserts for precision machining. Saudi Arabia imports all of these today despite having the mineral feedstock domestically.

The global technical ceramics market was valued at $10.2 billion in 2024 growing at 7.8% CAGR. A Saudi technical ceramics producer anchored by Ma'aden's alumina and serving defense, semiconductor, and industrial customers would be simultaneously a Vision 2030 industrial investment and a supply chain localisation for multiple PSAP sectors.

companies.name: CeramTec
companies.rationale: CeramTec is Europe's largest technical ceramics manufacturer with defence, medical, and electronics ceramic products. Non-ITAR German technology.

companies.name: Kyocera
companies.rationale: Kyocera produces technical ceramics for electronics and industrial applications and has international manufacturing partnerships.

companies.name: Saint-Gobain Performance Ceramics
companies.rationale: Saint-Gobain produces technical ceramics including ballistic ceramics and has active Middle East industrial engagement.

companies.name: 3M Advanced Materials
companies.rationale: 3M produces technical ceramics for electronics and defense. Technology licensing for non-ITAR commercial ceramic grades.

companies.name: Coorstek
companies.rationale: Coorstek manufactures industrial ceramics and has international facilities.
